generatedSentence: Nowadays, a palpable `suffering` hung over the `estuary`, where `janitors` and `pickers` toiled, their `poverty` a stark contrast to the `vintage` `projector` still running `glitchy` `ultrasounds` in the `apothecary` window, a `gimmick` to `typify` false hope; `mayhap` the `mayfly` lifespan of their `underwear` was a better `gander` at their future, marked by a `backlog` of `typhoid` scares and the `vanilla` routine of `washers` `jingles`, as the `taskbar` of life offered `nothing` but `vapory` dreams, leaving many `unready` for change amidst the encroaching `vegetation` and `trees` of the `jungle`. Whispers of `uprising` spread like `cancer` through the `international` district, a `scenario` of `insurgency` brewing against the `imperials` whose `ratings` soared from `grosses` profits, fueled by `barbarians` who would `hijack` any `radio` signal with `whacky` `offputting` `cowboy` `gunfire`, prompting a `downcast` `envoy` to deliver a `forewarned` `accusation` regarding a `tragedy`; the `raider` `brothers` sought `revenge` for `overhit` deals, their `unrated` `scope` now fixed on the `marquis` and his `monkey` pet, ready to `gobbles` up any `answers` or `perforation` in their defense. The `climax` exploded with a `surprise` `equalizer` blast, a `miraculous` `salvation` from a `charismatic`, `anointed` `virgin` whose `eyesight` cut through the `candellight`; armed with `craftsmanship` and a `crowbar`, she was a `breaker` against the `illiterate` `dwarves` who had `unpack`ed their `ketchup` and `baklava` feast, `seductively` offering `kisses` before the `gyroscope` of battle truly spun, allowing her to `transform` the `gums` of a crumbling wall into a shield, proving that `myself` can be mighty, even against the `opossum`-like tenacity of the enemy. In the `hindsight` of `victory`, the `mailbox` held `guarantee`s of `retention`, `torrents` of new `answers` for the `underpay`ed, a `marriage` of `varsity` spirit and practical `educate`ion, but the `frostbite` of past `suffering` remained a stark reminder; a `grackle` perched on a newly planted tree, an `eyewitness` to the monumental shift, symbolizing the fresh `vegetation` that now flourished where `mayhap` only `poverty` had been before.

In the `hindsight` of `victory`, the `mailbox` held `guarantee`s of `retention`, `torrents` of new `answers` for the `underpay`ed, a `marriage` of `varsity` spirit and practical `educate`ion, but the `frostbite` of past `suffering` remained a stark reminder; a `grackle` perched on a newly planted tree, an `eyewitness` to the monumental shift, symbolizing the fresh `vegetation` that now flourished where `mayhap` only `poverty` had been before.
rawPanelSentences: Nowadays, a palpable `suffering` hung over the `estuary`, where `janitors` and `pickers` toiled, their `poverty` a stark contrast to the `vintage` `projector` still running `glitchy` `ultrasounds` in the `apothecary` window, a `gimmick` to `typify` false hope; `mayhap` the `mayfly` lifespan of their `underwear` was a better `gander` at their future, marked by a `backlog` of `typhoid` scares and the `vanilla` routine of `washers` `jingles`, as the `taskbar` of life offered `nothing` but `vapory` dreams, leaving many `unready` for change amidst the encroaching `vegetation` and `trees` of the `jungle`.

Whispers of `uprising` spread like `cancer` through the `international` district, a `scenario` of `insurgency` brewing against the `imperials` whose `ratings` soared from `grosses` profits, fueled by `barbarians` who would `hijack` any `radio` signal with `whacky` `offputting` `cowboy` `gunfire`, prompting a `downcast` `envoy` to deliver a `forewarned` `accusation` regarding a `tragedy`; the `raider` `brothers` sought `revenge` for `overhit` deals, their `unrated` `scope` now fixed on the `marquis` and his `monkey` pet, ready to `gobbles` up any `answers` or `perforation` in their defense.

The `climax` exploded with a `surprise` `equalizer` blast, a `miraculous` `salvation` from a `charismatic`, `anointed` `virgin` whose `eyesight` cut through the `candellight`; armed with `craftsmanship` and a `crowbar`, she was a `breaker` against the `illiterate` `dwarves` who had `unpack`ed their `ketchup` and `baklava` feast, `seductively` offering `kisses` before the `gyroscope` of battle truly spun, allowing her to `transform` the `gums` of a crumbling wall into a shield, proving that `myself` can be mighty, even against the `opossum`-like tenacity of the enemy.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A lone female rebel with glowing bionic arm and a trench coat made of recycled circuit boards, her face partially obscured by a holographic visor. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist surveys a vast, ruined city with neon-lit debris scattered everywhere, from a high vantage point, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist confronts a group of heavily armored enforcers in a narrow, rain-slicked alley lit by garish holographic signs, eye-level shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, wielding a glowing crowbar, leaps through a torrent of digital data and broken circuitry against a giant, glitching server, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist stands triumphant amidst newly thriving, bioluminescent vegetation, looking towards a vibrant futuristic sunrise, over-the-shoulder shot.

definitions.definition: To rise; to get up; to appear from below the horizon.
To have an upward direction or inclination
To rebel or revolt; to take part in an uprising.
A popular revolt that attempts to overthrow a government or its policies; an insurgency or insurrection.
Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.
Underpants (boxers, briefs, panties, etc) and often bras.
A male goose.
A fool, simpleton.
(used only with “have”, “get” and “take”) A glance, look.
definitions.example: Have a gander at what he’s written.

definitions.definition: A man living apart from his wife.
Ramble, wander
Maybe, perhaps, possibly, perchance:
Any of the many fragile insects of the order Ephemeroptera that develop in fresh water and live very briefly as winged adults.
Any tropical, climbing orchid of the genus Vanilla (especially Vanilla planifolia), bearing podlike fruit yielding an extract used in flavoring food or in perfumes.
The fruit or bean of the vanilla plant.
The extract of the fruit of the vanilla plant.
The distinctive fragrant flavour/flavor characteristic of vanilla extract.
definitions.example: You can tell that the secret ingredient missing from New CokeTM was vanilla, because certain South American economies collapsed when it was introduced, and miraculously revived when the old formula was used again.

definitions.definition: Any artificially produced homologue of vanilla extract, principally vanillin produced from lignin from the paper industry or from petrochemicals.
(sexuality) Someone who is not into fetishism; a normophile
An unmodded version of a game
A yellowish-white colour, like that of vanilla ice cream.
(of flavor, etc.) Of vanilla.
(retronym) Standard, plain, default, unmodified, basic.

definitions.definition: (sexuality) Not kinky, not involving BDSM.
Plain; conventional; unimaginative.
Plants, taken collectively.
definitions.example: There were large amounts of vegetation in the forest.

definitions.definition: An abnormal verrucous or fibrinous growth
The act or process of vegetating, or growing as a plant does; vegetable growth.
University
The principal sports team representing an institution (usually a high school, college, or university.)
Any form of personal retaliatory action against an individual, institution, or group for some alleged or perceived harm or injustice.
definitions.example: Indifference is the sweetest revenge.

definitions.definition: A win by a previous loser.
To take revenge for (a particular harmful action) or on behalf of (its victim); to avenge.
definitions.example: Arsenal revenged its loss to Manchester United last time with a 5–0 drubbing this time.

definitions.definition: To take one's revenge (on or upon someone).
To take vengeance; to revenge itself.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
A touch with the lips, usually to express love or affection, or as a greeting.
An 'X' mark placed at the end of a letter or other type of message.
A type of filled chocolate candy, shaped as if someone had kissed the top. See Hershey's Kisses.
A tomato-vinegar-based sauce, sometimes containing spices, onion or garlic, and (especially in the US) sweeteners.
Such a sauce more generally (not necessarily based on tomatoes).
definitions.example: fish ketchup; fruit ketchup; mushroom ketchup

definitions.definition: To cover with ketchup.
A popular sweet pastry found in many cuisines of the Middle East and the Balkans, made of chopped nuts layered with phyllo pastry.
One who makes equal; a balancer.
A device that balances various quantities.
A goal, run, point, etc. that equalizes the score.
An electronic audio device for altering the frequencies of sound recordings.
A device, such as a bar, for operating two brakes, especially a pair of hub brakes for an automobile, with equal force.
Any device for equalizing the pull of electromagnets.
A conductor of low resistance joining the armature ends of the series field coils of dynamos connected in parallel.
A sliding panel to preserve the lateral stability of an aeroplane.
A set of arguments where two or more functions have equal values; the solution set of an equation.

A morphism whose codomain is the domain of a parallel pair of morphisms and which forms part of the limit of that parallel pair. Equivalently, a morphism which equalizes a parallel pair of morphisms in a limiting way, which is to say that any other morphism which equalizes that parallel pair factors through this limiting morphism; and moreover such factorization is unique.
A weapon, usually a blackjack or gun
Coastal water body where ocean tides and river water merge, resulting in a brackish water zone.
An ocean inlet also fed by fresh river water.
Something that washes; especially an appliance such as a washing machine or dishwasher.
A person who washes (especially clothes) for a living; a washerman or washerwoman.
A flat disk, placed beneath a nut or at some joint, to distribute pressure, alleviate friction or prevent leakage.
A face cloth.
To fit (a mechanical device) with a washer.
A response or reply; something said or done in reaction to a statement or question.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem.
definitions.example: There is no simple answer to corruption.

definitions.definition: A document filed in response to a complaint, responding to each point raised in the complaint and raising counterpoints.
To make a reply or response to.

definitions.definition: To speak in defence against; to reply to in defence.
definitions.example: to answer a charge or accusation

definitions.definition: To respond to a call by someone at a door or telephone, or other similar piece of equipment.

definitions.definition: To suit a need or purpose satisfactorily.

definitions.definition: To be accountable or responsible; to make amends.
definitions.example: He has a lot to answer for.

definitions.definition: To file a document in response to a complaint.
To correspond to; to be in harmony with; to be in agreement with.
To be opposite, or to act in opposition.
To be or act in conformity, or by way of accommodation, correspondence, relation, or proportion; to conform; to correspond; to suit; usually with to.
To respond to satisfactorily; to meet successfully by way of explanation, argument, or justification; to refute.
To be or act in compliance with, in fulfillment or satisfaction of, as an order, obligation, or demand.

definitions.definition: To render account to or for.
To atone for; to be punished for.
To be or act as an equivalent to, or as adequate or sufficient for; to serve for; to repay.
The sound of metal or glass clattering against itself.

definitions.definition: A memorable short song, or in some cases a snippet of a popular song with its lyrics modified, used for the purposes of advertising a product or service in a TV or radio commercial.

definitions.definition: A carriage drawn by horses.
A brief phone call; a ring.

definitions.definition: A jingle shell.
Coin money.

definitions.definition: To rhyme or sound with a jingling effect.
A large, undeveloped, humid forest, especially in a tropical region, that is home to many wild plants and animals; a tropical rainforest.
Any uncultivated tract of forest or scrub habitat.
A place where people behave ruthlessly, unconstrained by law or morality.

A style of electronic music related to drum and bass.

Dense rough.

definitions.definition: Any plant that is reminiscent of the above but not classified as a tree (in any botanical sense).

definitions.definition: An object made from a tree trunk and having multiple hooks or storage platforms.

definitions.definition: The structural frame of a saddle.
A connected graph with no cycles or, if the graph is finite, equivalently a connected graph with n vertices and n−1 edges.
A recursive data structure in which each node has zero or more nodes as children.
A display or listing of entries or elements such that there are primary and secondary entries shown, usually linked by drawn lines or by indenting to the right.

definitions.definition: Any structure or construct having branches representing divergence or possible choices.

Preflop, the position two before the dealer.

The act of perforating or the state of being perforated.

That portion of a surface that remains after an open disk is removed from it.

The bulk, the mass, the masses.

definitions.definition: A fanciful or whimsical person.

definitions.example: I would like to thank the brother who just spoke.

definitions.definition: A form of address to a man.
definitions.example: Listen, brother, I don't know what you want, but I'm not interested.

definitions.definition: A black male.
Somebody, usually male, connected by a common cause or situation.
Someone who is a peer, whether male or female.
To treat as a brother.
The quality or state of being poor; lack of money
Any deficiency of elements or resources that are needed or desired, or that constitute richness
definitions.example: poverty of soil

definitions.definition: Zany; eccentric
Disconcerting, annoying or repulsive; tending to put off.
definitions.example: His habit of picking his nose is quite off-putting.

definitions.definition: The state of being married.
definitions.example: You should enter marriage for love.

definitions.definition: A union of two or more people that creates a family tie and carries legal, social, and/or religious rights and responsibilities.
A homosexual relationship between male prisoners.
A wedding; a ceremony in which people wed.
definitions.example: You are cordially invited to the marriage of James Smith and Jane Doe.

definitions.definition: A close union.
A joining of two parts.
A king and a queen, when held as a hand in Texas hold 'em or melded in pinochle.
In solitaire or patience games, the placing a card of the same suit on the next one above or below it in value.
(reflexive pronoun) Me, as direct or indirect object the speaker as the object of a verb or preposition, when the speaker is also the subject.
definitions.example: I taught myself.

definitions.definition: Personally, for my part; used in apposition to I, sometimes for simple emphasis and sometimes with implicit exclusion of any others performing the activity described.
In my normal state of body or mind.
Me (as the object of a verb or preposition).
definitions.example: I feel like myself.

definitions.definition: I (as the subject of a verb).
My name is...
definitions.example: Myself John.

definitions.definition: Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).
definitions.example: Please don't monkey with the controls if you don't know what you're doing.

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
A person who makes and provides/sells drugs and/or medicines.
A drugstore or pharmacy.
A glass jar similar to those once used for medicine.
To embody, exemplify; to represent by an image, form, model, or resemblance.
definitions.example: His attitude typifies the attitude of young people today.

definitions.definition: To portray stereotypically.
definitions.example: The angry captain typified all new sailors as incompetent.

definitions.definition: To serve as a typical or reference specimen of.
definitions.example: The chosen specimen typifies plants belonging to that species.

definitions.definition: Sound with a frequency greater than the upper limit of human hearing, which is approximately 20 kilohertz.
The use of ultrasonic waves for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.
Fellatio; blowjob
An act of eating hastily or greedily.
To eat hastily or greedily; to scoff or scarf (often used with up)
definitions.example: He gobbled four hot dogs in three minutes.

definitions.definition: The sound of a turkey.
A rapid straight putt so strongly played that, if the ball had not gone into the hole, it would have gone a long way past.
To make the sound of a turkey.
The process of being saved, the state of having been saved (from hell).
definitions.example: Collective salvation is not possible without personal salvation, but the latter is achievable.

definitions.definition: The process of being restored or made new for the purpose of becoming saved; the process of being rid of the old poor quality conditions and becoming improved.
To save, in the religious sense; to bring to salvation.
The breadth, depth or reach of a subject; a domain.
A device used in aiming a projectile, through which the person aiming looks at the intended target.
Opportunity; broad range; degree of freedom.
The region of program source code in which a given identifier is meaningful, or a given object can be accessed.
The shortest sub-wff of which a given instance of a logical connective is a part.
The region of an utterance to which some modifying element applies.
definitions.example: the scope of an adverb

definitions.definition: A periscope, telescope, microscope or oscilloscope.
Any medical procedure that ends in the suffix -scopy, such as endoscopy, colonoscopy, bronchoscopy, etc.
To perform a cursory investigation of; scope out.
To perform any medical procedure that ends in the suffix -scopy, such as endoscopy, colonoscopy, bronchoscopy, etc.
definitions.example: The surgeon will scope the football player's knee to repair damage to a ligament.

definitions.definition: To limit (an object or variable) to a certain region of program source code.
definitions.example: If we locally scope the user's login name, it won't be accessible from outside this function.

definitions.definition: To examine under a microscope.
definitions.example: The entomologist explained that he could not tell what species of springtail we were looking at without scoping it.

definitions.definition: To observe a bird using a spotting scope.
By supernatural or uncommon causes, e.g. by a god; that cannot be explained in terms of normal events.
Very surprising; amazing.
The act of retaining or something retained
The act or power of remembering things
A memory; what is retained in the mind
The involuntary withholding of urine and faeces
The length of time an individual remains in treatment
That which contains something, as a tablet; a means of preserving impressions.
The act of withholding; restraint; reserve.
A place of custody or confinement.
The right to withhold a debt, or of retaining property until a debt due to the person claiming the right is duly paid; a lien.
A violent flow, as of water, lava, etc.; a stream suddenly raised and running rapidly, as down a precipice.
definitions.example: A torrent of green and white water broke over the hull of the sail-boat.

definitions.definition: A large amount or stream of something.
definitions.example: They endured a torrent of inquiries.

definitions.definition: (file sharing) A set of files obtainable through a peer-to-peer network, especially BitTorrent.
definitions.example: I got a torrent of the complete works of Shakespeare the other day; I'm not sure why.

definitions.definition: To pay (someone) less than the value of their work; to pay (someone) insufficiently.
To pay less than is due for (something).
definitions.example: The underpaid letter was returned to the sender.

definitions.definition: Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.
A trivial remark (especially in the term sweet nothings).
A nobody (insignificant person).
definitions.example: You're nothing to me now!

definitions.definition: Not at all; in no way.
Not any thing; no thing.
An absence of anything, including empty space, brightness, darkness, matter, or a vacuum.
Something unexpected.
definitions.example: It was a surprise to find out I owed twice as much as I thought I did.

definitions.definition: The feeling that something unexpected has happened.
definitions.example: Imagine my surprise on learning I owed twice as much as I thought I did.

definitions.definition: A dish covered with a crust of raised pastry, but with no other contents.
To cause (someone) to feel unusually alarmed or delighted by something unexpected.
definitions.example: It surprises me that I owe twice as much as I thought I did.

definitions.definition: To do something to (a person) that they are not expecting, as a surprise.
definitions.example: He doesn’t know that I’m in the country – I thought I’d turn up at his house and surprise him.

definitions.definition: To undergo or witness something unexpected.
definitions.example: He doesn’t surprise easily.

definitions.definition: To cause surprise.
To attack unexpectedly.
To take unawares.
To warn in advance.
A person who has never had sexual intercourse, or sometimes, one who has never engaged in any sexual activity at all.
(early Christian Church) a woman noted for religious piety who has never been married.
One who has never used or experienced a specified thing.
definitions.example: I've never eaten tofu before – you could say I'm a tofu virgin.

definitions.definition: Any of several species of gossamer-winged butterflies of the family Lycaenidae.
A female insect producing eggs from which young are hatched, though there has been no fecundation by a male; a parthenogenetic insect.
(usually not comparable) In a state of virginity; chaste, not having had sexual intercourse.
Of a physical object, untouched.
Not yet cultivated, explored, or exploited by humans or humans of certain civilizations.
definitions.example: The virgin lands of the Americas were awaiting the Europeans.

definitions.definition: Of olive oil, obtained by mechanical means, so that the oil is not altered.
(usually not comparable) Of mixed drinks, not containing alcohol.
definitions.example: a virgin daiquiri

definitions.definition: Vision or the faculty of sight.
View or the range of vision.
To smear or rub over with oil or an unctuous substance; also, to spread over, as oil.
To apply oil to or to pour oil upon, etc., as a sacred rite, especially for consecration.
To choose or nominate somebody for a leading or otherwise important position, especially formally or officially, or as an intended successor.
To mark somebody as an official ruler, especially a king or queen, as a part of a religious ceremony.
A person who has been anointed, especially for religious reasons
Having undergone the process of anointing.
Utter; thorough; eminent.
A member of the Charismatic Movement.
Of, related to, or having charisma.
Of, related to, or being a member of a form of Christianity that emphasises the role of the Holy Spirit.
An illiterate person, one not able to read and write.
A person ignorant about a given subject.
definitions.example: The government is run by business illiterates.

definitions.definition: Unable to read and write.
Having less than an expected standard of familiarity with language and literature, or having little formal education.
Not conforming to prescribed standards of speech or writing.
Ignorant in a specified way or about a specified subject.
definitions.example: economically illiterate, emotionally illiterate

definitions.definition: A bottle of wine (usually Bordeaux) containing 6 liters of fluid, eight times the volume of a standard bottle.
A writing paper size measuring 30 × 22 inches, or printing paper measuring 32 × 22 inches.
A card game differing from piquet in some minor details, and in having a trump.
Any of several combinations of cards which score in this game.
A crown imperial.
A tuft of hair on the lower lip (so called from its use by Napoleon III).
A kind of dome, as in Moorish buildings.
An outside seat on a diligence.
A variety of green tea.
Any American marsupial of the family Didelphidae. The common species of the United States is Didelphis virginiana.
To remove the rating from something.
definitions.example: I'm annoyed that Netflix won't let me unrate that movie.

definitions.definition: Not rated; having no rating
Shots from a gun or guns, typically creating loud report.
definitions.example: Let's hide in the trees to avoid the gunfire.

definitions.definition: The use of gunpowder-type weapons, mainly cannon, as opposed to swords or bayonets.
definitions.example: Killing people became much easier and faster once armies started using gunfire.

definitions.definition: The time of firing of the morning gun or the evening gun.
Tea, a cup of tea, especially one served early in the morning before first parade.
A box into which mail is put
A folder or account for the storage of e-mail; an electronic in-box or mailstore.
An injury suffered as a result of freezing of some part of the body, typically fingers, toes or the nose.
To expose to the effect of frost, or a frosty air; to blight or nip with frost.
To engage in winter sailboating.
Something that breaks.
A machine for breaking rocks, or for breaking coal at the mines
The building in which such a machine is placed.
A person who specializes in breaking things.
(chiefly in the plural) A wave breaking into foam against the shore, or against a sandbank, or a rock or reef near the surface, considered a useful warning to ships of an underwater hazard
A breakdancer.
A user of CB radio.
One who works in shipbreaking.
Someone who sees an event and can report or testify about it.
To be present at an event, and see it
A short stanza at the end of a poem, used either to address a person or to comment on the preceding body of the poem.
A diplomatic agent of the second rank, next in status after an ambassador.
A representative.
A diplomat.
A messenger.
Someone who looks after the maintenance and cleaning of a public building.
A doorman.
A drama or similar work, in which the main character is brought to ruin or otherwise suffers the extreme consequences of some tragic flaw or weakness of character.
The genre of such works, and the art of producing them.
A disastrous event, especially one involving great loss of life or injury.
An iron or steel bar, often with a flattened end which may also be hook-shaped, to be used as a lever to manually force things apart.
An electrical circuit that prevents an overvoltage from causing damage.
A type of cocktail made with only Crown Royal whiskey and lemon lime soda.
To use force to move. To prise.
To hit too far or too hard.
definitions.example: The golfer overhit his shot onto the green, and it rolled into the bunker.

definitions.definition: Any of several American blackbirds of the genus Quiscalus, and related genera, having iridescent plumage.
(formerly) Any of several Asian myna birds of the genus Gracula.
At the present time; in the current era.

A nobleman in England, France, and Germany, of a rank next below that of duke, but above a count. Originally, the marquis was an officer whose duty was to guard the marches or frontiers of the kingdom. The office has ceased, and the name is now a mere title conferred by letters patent or letters close.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Asian genus Bassarona.
An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.
definitions.example: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

definitions.definition: The condition of having succeeded in a conflict or other effort.
A winged figure representing victory, common in Roman official iconography. See Winged victory.
To achieve a victory
Realisation or understanding of the significance and nature of events after they have occurred
definitions.example: In hindsight, I should have quit that job sooner.

definitions.definition: The rear sight of a firearm
To instruct or train
definitions.example: Wang said such changes to the Baishui glacier provide the chance to educate visitors about global warming.

definitions.definition: Agent noun of pick; one who picks.
definitions.example: The apple picker climbed the tree.

definitions.definition: Any user interface control that selects something.
definitions.example: a date picker

definitions.definition: A machine for picking fibrous materials to pieces so as to loosen and separate the fibre.
The piece in a loom that strikes the end of the shuttle and impels it through the warp.
A priming wire for cleaning the vent, in ordnance.
(gold panning) A fragment of gold smaller than a nugget but large enough to be picked up.
One who removes defects from and finishes electrotype plates.
A pilferer.
An operation (often an integration) that converts one function into another.
A function so produced.
To change greatly the appearance or form of.
definitions.example: The alchemists sought to transform lead into gold.

definitions.definition: To change the nature, condition or function of; to change in nature, disposition, heart, character, etc.; to convert.
To subject to a transformation; to change into another form without altering the value.
To subject to the action of a transformer.
To subject (a cell) to transformation.
To undergo a transformation; to change in appearance or character.
A cast from supertype to subtype.
A melancholy look.
A ventilating shaft down which the air passes in circulating through a mine.
To cast or throw down; to turn downward.
To taunt; to reproach; to upbraid.
To cast from supertype to subtype.
(of eyes) Looking downwards.
(of a person) Feeling despondent.

Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.
A person of short stature, often one whose limbs are disproportionately small in relation to the body as compared with normal adults, usually as the result of a genetic condition.
An animal, plant or other thing much smaller than the usual of its sort.
definitions.example: dwarf honeysuckle

definitions.definition: A star of relatively small size.
Not ready.
Anything that assures a certain outcome.
definitions.example: Can you give me a guarantee that he will be fit for the match?

definitions.definition: A legal assurance of something, e.g. a security for the fulfillment of an obligation.
More specifically, a written declaration that a certain product will be fit for a purpose and work correctly; a warranty
definitions.example: The cooker comes with a five-year guarantee.

definitions.definition: The person to whom a guarantee is made.
A person who gives such a guarantee; a guarantor.
To give an assurance that something will be done right.
To assume or take responsibility for a debt or other obligation.
To make something certain.
definitions.example: The long sunny days guarantee a good crop.
